description: Original tasks before rewriting based on actual data

tasks.name: Save the top 2 reclaimed wood wall art projects under 3 hours to a new collection
tasks.description: Find wall art projects that use reclaimed wood with a maximum build time of 3 hours, sort them by rating, and save the top 2 results into a new collection named 'Quick Reclaimed Wall Art'. Success is achieved when that collection contains exactly those two projects.
steps: Navigate to the website homepage.
In the main navigation menu, click on the 'Projects' section.
In the project category filters on the left sidebar, click on 'Wall Art'.
In the material filter section, select the checkbox for 'Reclaimed Wood'.
In the build time filter, set the maximum time to '3 hours' and apply the filter.
Open the sort dropdown above the project results and select 'Rating: High to Low'.

Click on the first project card in the sorted results to open its project page in the current tab, then use the browser back button to return to the results and repeat for the second project so you have both project pages open in two separate tabs.
On the first selected project page, click the 'Save to Collection' or 'Add to Collection' button and choose the option to create a new collection named 'Quick Reclaimed Wall Art'.
On the second selected project page, click the 'Save to Collection' or 'Add to Collection' button and add the project to the existing 'Quick Reclaimed Wall Art' collection.

tasks.name: Comment on the highest-rated beginner wood burning tutorial from the last 3 months
tasks.description: Search for wood burning tutorials, filter for beginner difficulty and posts from the last 3 months, sort by rating, open the top result, and post a comment with a specific message. Success is achieved when that tutorial has your comment added.
steps: Navigate to the website homepage.
Click on the search bar in the header.
Type 'wood burning tutorial' into the search field and press Enter.
On the search results page, use the content type filter to select only 'Tutorial' posts.
Use the difficulty filter and select 'Beginner'.
Open the date range filter and set it to show posts from the last '3 months', then apply the filter.
Open the sort dropdown and select 'Rating: High to Low'.
Click on the first tutorial in the sorted list to open its detail page.
Scroll to the comments section, click in the comment text field, type the message 'Planning to try this technique this weekend!' and click the 'Post Comment' or 'Submit' button.

tasks.name: Post a new question in the 'Beginner Tools' forum about chisels under $100
tasks.description: Use the forum to search for existing threads about beginner chisel sets, then create a new topic in the 'Beginner Tools' subforum with a specific title and content asking for recommendations under $100. Success is achieved when the new topic is posted in the correct subforum with the specified title.
steps: From any page, open the main navigation menu and click on 'Community' or 'Forum'.
In the forum category list, click on the 'Beginner Tools' subforum.
In the subforum page, locate the search field for threads and type 'first chisel set under 100' and press Enter.

Scroll through the thread titles on the search results page to review existing topics and then click the 'Back' or 'Beginner Tools' breadcrumb link to return to the main 'Beginner Tools' subforum page.
On the 'Beginner Tools' subforum page, click the 'New Topic' or 'Start New Thread' button.
In the topic title field, type 'Advice on my first chisel set under $100'.

In the main post content textarea, type a short message describing your situation, including the sentence 'I am looking for a beginner-friendly chisel set under $100 for small woodworking art projects.'
If there is a tag or category field for the topic, type and select 'chisels' and 'beginner' as tags.
Click the 'Post Topic' or 'Create Thread' button to publish your question in the 'Beginner Tools' subforum.

tasks.name: Create a gift wishlist of 3 wall art pieces under $60 each with total under $150
tasks.description: In the marketplace, find wooden wall art pieces with ratings of at least 4.5 stars and individual prices under $60, then add exactly 3 items whose combined price is not more than $150 to a wishlist named 'Gift Ideas'. Success is achieved when the 'Gift Ideas' wishlist contains exactly 3 qualifying items within the budget.
steps: Navigate to the website homepage.
In the main navigation, click on the 'Marketplace' or 'Shop' section.
In the category filters, select 'Wall Art' as the product category.
Use the price filter to set the maximum price per item to '$60' and apply the filter.
Use the rating filter to show only items with ratings of '4.5 stars and up'.
Open the sort dropdown and select 'Price: Low to High'.

Starting from the first item in the sorted list, open product pages in new tabs and choose items so that you select exactly 3 pieces whose combined prices do not exceed $150, then on each of these 3 product pages click 'Add to Wishlist' or 'Save', and create or choose a wishlist named 'Gift Ideas'.
If prompted to name a new wishlist, type 'Gift Ideas' as the wishlist name and confirm the creation when adding the first item, then add the remaining chosen items to the same 'Gift Ideas' wishlist.

tasks.name: Build a beginner practice reading list with 2 carving and 2 joinery posts under 20 minutes
tasks.description: Find beginner-level blog posts in the 'Carving' and 'Joinery' categories, each with an estimated reading time of 20 minutes or less, and add exactly 2 from each category to a reading list named 'Beginner Practice Plan'. Success is achieved when the reading list contains 4 posts: 2 carving and 2 joinery.
steps: From the homepage, click on the 'Blog' or 'Articles' section in the main navigation.
In the category filter, select 'Carving'.
Use the difficulty filter to select 'Beginner'.
Use the reading time filter or slider to set the maximum reading time to '20 minutes' and apply the filter.

Open the first two articles in the filtered list in new tabs and on each article page click 'Add to Reading List' or 'Save', then create a new reading list named 'Beginner Practice Plan' when adding the first carving article.
Return to the blog or articles section, change the category filter from 'Carving' to 'Joinery' while keeping 'Beginner' and the '20 minutes or less' filters applied.

Open the first two joinery articles in the filtered list in new tabs and on each article page click 'Add to Reading List' or 'Save', this time choosing the existing 'Beginner Practice Plan' reading list.

If there is a reading lists page in your profile or account menu, navigate to it and click on 'Beginner Practice Plan' to confirm that all 4 selected posts have been added by viewing the list contents.

tasks.name: Follow 2 top geometric-style artists and enable notifications for their new projects
tasks.description: Use the artist directory to find geometric-style woodworking artists with at least 5 completed projects, sort by follower count, then follow the top 2 and enable in-site notifications for their new project posts. Success is achieved when both artists are followed and notifications are turned on for each.
steps: From any page, open the main navigation menu and click on the 'Artists' or 'Creators' directory.
In the style or theme filter, select 'Geometric' as the style.
Use the projects count filter to set a minimum of '5 completed projects' and apply the filter.
Open the sort dropdown and choose 'Most Followers' or 'Popularity: High to Low'.
Click on the first artist in the sorted list to open their profile in a new tab, then repeat for the second artist so that you have the top 2 artist profiles open.
On the first artist's profile page, click the 'Follow' button.
On the same profile page, locate the notification settings or bell icon for this artist and click it to enable notifications specifically for 'New Projects' or 'New Posts'.
Switch to the second artist's profile tab, click the 'Follow' button, and then click the notification settings or bell icon to enable notifications for their 'New Projects' or 'New Posts' as well.

tasks.name: Create a materials list from the 'Floating Shelf with Hidden Mounting' project
tasks.description: Find the 'Floating Shelf with Hidden Mounting' project and copy all listed materials into a project materials list using the site's materials-list feature. Success is achieved when all materials from that project are added to a materials list named 'Floating Shelf Build List'.
steps: Navigate to the website homepage.
Click on the search bar in the header and type 'Floating Shelf with Hidden Mounting', then press Enter.
In the search results, click on the project titled 'Floating Shelf with Hidden Mounting' to open its project page.
On the project page, click on the 'Materials' tab or scroll to the section labeled 'Materials' or 'Materials List'.
If there is an option to 'Create Materials List' or 'Add All to List', click it and create a new list named 'Floating Shelf Build List'.

If you need to add items individually, for each material listed (e.g., wood boards, brackets, screws), click the 'Add to Materials List' button next to the material and choose or create the 'Floating Shelf Build List' for each one.
After adding all materials, open the 'Materials Lists' or 'My Lists' section from your profile or account menu.

In the lists section, click on 'Floating Shelf Build List' to view the list and confirm that it contains all materials from the 'Floating Shelf with Hidden Mounting' project by reviewing the item names.

tasks.name: Register for the cheapest beginner wood carving online workshop next month under $40
tasks.description: Use the events or workshops section to find online beginner wood carving workshops scheduled for next calendar month, filter for prices of $40 or less, sort by price, select the cheapest option, and complete the registration form using the 'Pay Later' or equivalent non-external payment option. Success is achieved when the registration for that specific workshop is submitted.
steps: From the homepage, navigate to the 'Events' or 'Workshops' section via the main navigation menu.
In the event type filter, select 'Online' workshops only.
Use the topic or category filter to choose 'Wood Carving'.
Use the difficulty filter and select 'Beginner'.
Open the date filter and set it to show events occurring in the next full calendar month (for example, if it is March now, set the range to April 1 to April 30), then apply the filter.
Use the price filter to set the maximum event price to '$40' and apply the filter.
Open the sort dropdown and choose 'Price: Low to High', then click on the first workshop result in the sorted list to open its detail page.
On the workshop detail page, click the 'Register' or 'Sign Up' button.

In the registration form, fill in the required text fields (for example, enter 'Alex Wood' in the name field, 'Springfield' in the city field, and 'Beginner hobbyist' in a notes or experience field if present), select the 'Pay Later', 'Pay at Workshop', or any non-external payment method in the payment options, and click the final 'Submit Registration' or 'Confirm' button.
